title: Red flags: the setups we refuse to take
_note: EVERGREEN (warm-up bank for ad traffic). No invented numbers/prices/dates.
body:
✅ #BeforeYouTrade — the setups we refuse to take

Knowing what NOT to trade is half the edge. Here's our no-list. If a setup trips any one of these, it doesn't ship — no matter how good it looks.

☐ SL would sit inside a liquidation magnet. If the only stop placement lands right on a heavy cluster from the heatmap, a routine sweep takes us out of a thesis that was still valid. Bad stop = no trade.

☐ Major news inside ~10 minutes. A scheduled print or event about to drop turns price into a coin flip. We don't hold size into a known volatility spike. Wait for the dust, then read the real move.

☐ Chasing after a big move. The candle already ran. Entering now means buying the crowd's exit at the worst price. The move you wish you'd caught is not a setup — it's a memory. Let it go.

☐ No clear invalidation. If we can't point to a single price where the idea is simply wrong, there's no stop, no sizing, no trade. 'It should work' is not an invalidation.

☐ Funding neutral + no confluence. Nothing extreme, nothing lined up, fewer than 2 confirmations agreeing. That's not a signal — it's noise asking to be traded. The answer is no.

None of these mean the market is 'unbeatable today'. They mean THIS setup isn't ours. We'd rather sit flat through ten of these than force one and hand the crowd our liquidity.

The best trade is the one you skip. Most days, that's most of them.

#BeforeYouTrade
